# UC-12.7.7 · Ansible AWX Job Template Concurrent Execution Queue Depth

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Capacity, Performance &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch queueing and wait time so developers are not stuck behind broken runners or clogged pipelines.*

---

## Description

AWX serializes work across instance groups; a growing pending queue means execution capacity, database performance, or container limits are saturated. Early detection preserves SLA for patching and compliance runs that must finish overnight.

## Value

Avoids surprise job backlog during patch Tuesday and informs horizontal scaling of execution planes.

## Implementation

Poll `/api/v2/metrics/` or custom telemetry job that emits pending_jobs and running_jobs per controller. Tag `instance_group`. Correlate with PostgreSQL latency and container CPU on execution nodes.

## SPL

```spl
index=iac sourcetype="ansible:awx"
| eval pending=coalesce(pending_jobs, queue_pending, jobs_pending)
| eval running=coalesce(running_jobs, jobs_running)
| where isnotnull(pending)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats max(pending) as max_pending, max(running) as max_running, latest(instance_group) as ig by _time, controller_host
| where max_pending > 25
| sort -max_pending
```

## Visualization

Area chart (pending vs running over time), single-value gauge (max pending), table by instance group.

## Known False Positives

Build times vary with cache state, runner availability, or test suite expansion.

## References

- [Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform — Metrics](https://docs.ansible.com/automation-platform/latest/html/administration/metrics.html)
